mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 12-month forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that sustains corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward windows, or RSAFS and HOUST show divergent trends that nullify the predictive power (interaction term p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals weakening manufacturing output that compresses profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 70% of observations where INDPRO falls below its 12-month MA, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during recovery phases (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases borrowing costs and equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions (Mann-Whitney U p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE levels yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that directly translates into higher corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals an immediate supply-side shock that disrupts supply chains and reduces near-term earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO contracts by > 0.5%, or the signal fails to predict returns during contraction phases (Rank IC < 0.01).
